plan.feature_summary: Real-time collaborative document editor with multiple user simultaneous editing, full revision history, and role-based access

tasks.title: Implement user authentication
tasks.description: Implement user authentication using FastAPI and PostgreSQL
tasks.code_stub:
def authenticate_user(username: str, password: str) -> bool:
    # Purpose: Authenticate user
    # Args: username (str), password (str)
    # Returns: bool
    # TODO: implement authentication logic
    user = User.query.filter_by(username=username).first()
    return user.check_password(password)

tasks.title: Create document model
tasks.description: Design and implement document model with revision history
tasks.code_stub:
def create_document(title: str, content: str) -> Document:
    # Purpose: Create new document
    # Args: title (str), content (str)
    # Returns: Document
    # TODO: implement document creation logic
    document = Document(title=title, content=content)
    document.save()

acceptance_criteria: Document is created with revision history
Document is returned in API response

tasks.title: Implement role-based access control
tasks.description: Implement role-based access control for documents
tasks.code_stub:
def check_access(document_id: int, user_id: int) -> bool:
    # Purpose: Check user access to document
    # Args: document_id (int), user_id (int)
    # Returns: bool
    # TODO: implement access control logic
    document = Document.query.get(document_id)
    user = User.query.get(user_id)
    return user.has_access(document)
acceptance_criteria: Users can only access documents based on their role
Access control is enforced for all document operations

plan.sprint_recommendation: Execute tasks in the following order: T-001, T-002, T-003, T-005, T-004. Tasks T-002 and T-003 can be executed in parallel. Task T-004 depends on T-002 and T-003, and should be executed last.
unknowns: How will the team handle conflicts between multiple users editing the same document simultaneously?
What is the expected load on the system, and how will it be handled?
